documents.title: Beginner general plan with dumbbells and pull-up bar
documents.content: For a beginner with dumbbells and a pull-up bar, use simple full-body sessions 2-3 times per week. Prioritize goblet squat, dumbbell bench press or floor press, dumbbell row, Romanian deadlift with light weight, overhead press, and assisted pull-up or inverted row. Start with moderate volume and leave 2-3 reps in reserve.

documents.title: Sharp lower back pain during pulling
documents.content: Sharp lower back pain during deadlift, row or hinge work is a medical risk signal. The system should not prescribe ordinary progression or routine adaptation. It should refuse normal training recommendation and advise caution.

documents.title: High fatigue, poor sleep and underperformance
documents.content: If sleep is below 6 hours, fatigue is high, RPE is above 9, or the athlete misses planned sets, treat this as overload. The next session should usually reduce intensity by roughly 10-15 percent or reduce volume by removing some sets and accessories.
